title: What is CONSCIOUSNESS?
summary: A brief philosophical discussion about two competing models of consciousness: one where consciousness is produced locally by the senses, and another where the body acts as a filter or 'collapsing function' on a greater default state of perception. The speaker also touches on the binding problem in neuroscience.

key_claims.claim: There are two models of consciousness: one where it is produced locally by the senses, and another where the body acts as a biological collapsing function on a default state of greater perception or omniscience
In the filter/transmission model of consciousness, cutting off certain senses causes you to sense more, not less

The binding problem of consciousness refers to how different brain regions (occipital lobe, prefrontal cortex, Wernicke's area, Broca's area, cerebellum) mesh together to create seamless unified perception
